content:
Hey <@1376763322568937562> , could we have your opinion (and maybe the narrative team's opinion) on the choice of "Awakener" as a term.

My guess is that this is foreshadowing what happens in ch8/9.

We have opened the discussion for the French term that should be used, and we were wondering what things need to be considered to maybe choose a more appropriate terms than "*Réveilleur*", since it is a bit clunky.
The main point being : **what are the essential concepts we need to convey with the term used** ?
Just noticed the terms sheet doesn't include Kathigu-Ra, her SSR/SR Wheels, Skills or anything related to her, really. Maybe you should add those.

+1
her second skill card has a typo that I looked for
and we have the text for her banner

Since there already is a separate Japanese version fully translated will it be ported over to the global client? I play in Japanese on the global client. If not, we could literally just copy their homework since they did the work.

I've also played the Japanese version, but I feel like there are some rough spots in the translation.
Of course, it depends on the wishes of the developers, but I think it would be better to use the translation as a reference to some extent and then make further improvements rather than porting it completely.

We as devs cannot use our Japanese publisher's translation in the global version, like we can't just mass copy their stuff on our end... unfortunately...
Whether you want to use Morimens JP's translation as reference is up to you to decide!😄

There are now two examples of Carl-bot swiftly deleting 40+ minutes of detailed discussion work that is important for this group project to come to important consensuses on how to move forward, simply due to a single casual use of the f-word.

It has not left any trace of the frankly exorbitant amount of labor put in to the message that was deleted, leaving us absolutely no option but to attempt to retrace our thoughts and rebuild the entire page of statements from scratch.

The profanity filter does not appear to apply to Moderators, so both examples of this have come from <#1407967196495482931> in which Goz used the f-word, which gave us the impression that it was allowed to be used. I find selective application of the filter to be unjust, and if anyone is allowed to write freely, **everyone** should have that privelege. We're all adults here.

More importantly, a MUCH more relevant argument is that this is a multi-lingual server, and the risk is therefore multiplied **exponentially** by the fact that we don't know what exactly the profanity filter is looking for, and **every** language makes regular use of words or parts of words that would be considered profanity in another language.

The risk of continued automatic deletion of valuable volunteer labor, with absolutely no recourse, dictated entirely by the whims of an unnecessary black-box profanity filter is frankly unacceptable and should be removed immediately.

# Please disable the profanity filter from Carl-Bot.
thread_title: Please disable profanity filter from Carl-Bot

content: Thank you! It was on by default, I'll look into how to disable it

content: Hi. I just got a little bit confused seeing Helot name being translated as Хилот (literally the same name but different writing). As i know Helot name is connected with Helots as dependent population enslaved by ancient Sparta. In this case, the name would be correctly localized as Илот (not Хелот). I don't know who does the translation of names so I'll just leave it at that.
About Helot's ru name
Yes, corrected. Thank you!

<@1376763322568937562> I don't wanna bug you *too* much so this'll be the last time I'll ping you about this (I wasn't sure if you caught my last messages) 
If you have time, could you please give your opinion on <#1407967196495482931>? There's a LOT there but it's important to the work that I'm planning to do, and the other debater Goz is offline at time of writing.

I also wanted to ask about exactly how much freedom I'm allowed to exercise in terms of reformatting the text of cards. Is it acceptable (for obvious example) for me to execute the kind of wide, sweeping changes we're discussing in that thread?

What verbs are and aren't on the table for me to change? Obviously anything in IMPORTANT TERMS as well as any unique Nouns and such like Aliemus and Orison are not to be touched *(not that I would want to, whoever came up with those is brilliant)* but is it important at all, for example, that Awakeners "obtain" Aliemus, or would it be fine for me to standardize all Aliemus increasing effects to match, either using "**gain**" (subjective) or "**grant**" (objective)?

I'm currently working on drafting a summary of the templating changes I intend to implement, so that they can be OK'd before I begin working them in, I should have that ready tomorrow.
Hello!
I'm still trying to backread everything in the STR bonus thread! I just got back from a meeting and I've got a couple other deadlines to meet today, sorry for my late response!

***Is it acceptable (for obvious example) for me to execute the kind of wide, sweeping changes we're discussing in that thread?***

I've been thinking about this, and I also asked for Light's opinion.
What you guys are doing is really cool! It would be great to have the current skill descs refined further by card game experts like you in our team. We are incredibly grateful.

I told Light that a Magic the Gathering Judge is offering to help us make all the skill descriptions better, he's really happy about this! Our combat designers are excited when I translated your discussion thread for them!

But, **let's not do sweeping improvements yet.**  For now, let's do it in a **"if not entirely broken, don't fix"** way; 
That is: If a skill description isn't intelligible/obviously just WRONG, let's keep it as is... for now! 

HERE'S WHY:
1) According to our combat designers, the current **original Chinese skill descriptions** in game are... messy and needs standardizing too. 😹 . They have wanted to improve the original Chinese version for a long time.
I see you're already working on a summary of templating changes. That's awesome! **I will translate it into Chinese so our combat designers can read! **

But, also because of this: 
It would be better to do English large-scale improvements after the Chinese is updated and further standardized.

Also:

1) The sheer amount of skill text could be an issue.
There are tens of thousands of lines of skill description texts. 
Let's say 10% of them is changed in the next update but the other 90% are still the same. This may confuse players further. 

2) Skill descriptions are more sensitive because people pay for units.
In a way, we can let story translation have errors and fix them later. But I will have to check every line of volunteer-edited skill desc to make sure no mistranslation happens (because most of our volunteers don't speak Chinese). 
Sometimes even I get confused, despite speaking Chinese. Only our combat designers are entirely sure about the skill descriptions they wrote.

--For example, **that Awakeners "obtain" Aliemus, or would it be fine for me to standardize all Aliemus increasing effects to match, either using "gain" (subjective) or "grant" (objective)?**

I think this type of standardizing is safe to do!
Our combat designers are now talking about how they wish they could rewrite all the original Chinese skill descriptions lol
I think your summary of all the standardizing you intend to do would be really helpful!!

To sum up:

1) For now: Let's **not do large sweeping edits yet**.
2) I will collect feedback & suggestions for improvements from you and forward it to our devs! 
Our lead combat designer agreed that they can start further standardizing everything **after the Second Anniversary.**

Like for example, they will read your suggestions, and improve the orignal Chinese skill descriptions first.
Then, I can work with you guys from there by translating the new Chinese descriptions into English again.

Okay, awesome! Thanks for the confidence boost haha
Here's what I'll plan to do, then.

1. Isolate, compile, and organize all of the lines I'll be changing, so it's easier for me to see exactly how big of a project I'm working with.
2. Create a vocabulary of terms, verbs, and phrases to be applied to the templating of gameplay effects, akin to a style guide.
3. Start working and submit my changes in absolute chunks, so that if, for example, the relic descriptions and skill cards don't use the same terms right away, at least the relic descriptions will all be corrected at once as a whole.
4. Work simultaneously on the small "let's just make this not improper english" edits **and** the "this is how it should eventually be templated" edits and keep them as separate sheets; I don't mind doing a lot of work and having very little of it go live until I'm finished, so long as the end result is perfect, and I'd rather not go through the entire workload multiple times, so if I'm analyzing every line of text anyway I'd like to be thorough the first time through.
5. Allowing for that work to inform how I write my templating summary, publish that summary alongside a sufficient amount of real, finished examples of how I would implement the large, sweeping changes, and detailed breakdowns of how and why I came to those conclusions.
6. (optional, if the designers are interested) Author a detailed list of fundamental UI and visual design suggestions that can help streamline and increase readability of gameplay elements moving forward. *(one major example is giving affixes such as Exhaust, Sluggish, Delirium, Mutation etc designated real estate on cards as opposed to being rich text tags appended to the description, which I might just cook up anyway because whether they want it or not it's a good idea)*

I figure I may as well, since I'm going through everything anyway, at least finish the work of rewriting everything the way I suggest it be templated in English and allow the finished product to help inform the Chinese descriptions, no? I really don't mind going back over that work far later down the line if the designers finalize chinese iterations on my finished work in a way that necessitates changes to that work.

but yes, **I'll make my first pull requests be basic fixes to vocabulary and corrections of typos and improper grammar, while slowly accumulating progress on the bigger picture behind the scenes to go live all at once.**
Thank you so much for this exciting opportunity!!

Is there any difference between them?
I found both of them being used in relic effects like

- 最大算力提高 [Arg1] 。    
- Max Arithmetica increased by [Arg1].

- 算力上限 + [Arg1] ， <Energy:[Energy:Arg2]> 狂气。    
- Arithmetica Limit + [Arg1], <Energy:[Energy:Arg2]> Aliemus.

If it is absolutely same, I want to unify the term in our translation.

content: This is a Ryker's pvp card. I looked everywhere, all pocker terms to see where it comes from, maybe it was a set of card, maybe like "all-in". But i couldn't find anything. So I'm here to ask - what does is that means, so I'll translate it peacefully without worrying that i missed a point

The chinese is 十环出牌！, which is also "ten rings". Outside of his PVP card, there seem to be no other mentions of 十环, so it doesn't seem to be related to anything else in his kit.

googling ten rings just gets me references to a Marvel movie of the same name, so it might be a reference to that? unsure, though...
